meta.notes: Self-hosted AI agent gateway. Connects messaging channels (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, iMessage, Signal) to AI agents. Requires Node 22+.
meta.tldr: This release introduces Codex Computer Use setup with status/install commands and marketplace discovery, along with DeepInfra as a new bundled provider offering model discovery, media generation, TTS, and embeddings. Tencent Yuanbao and QQBot support expands channel coverage with Yuanbao docs and QQBot group chat features. Plugin startup and model catalogs now use manifest-first metadata, streamlining Gateway boot. A new operator-managed outbound proxy routing feature is available via `proxy.enabled` and `proxy.proxyUrl` or the `OPENCLAW_PROXY_URL` environment variable. The `sandbox.docker.gpus` option enables GPU passthrough for Docker sandbox containers.  The `openclaw cron add` and `openclaw cron edit` commands now support the `--thread-id` flag for Telegram forum topic delivery. Reliability fixes address Telegram startup, Slack stalls, and Windows restart issues.  Legacy `CLAWDBOT_*` and `MOLTBOT_*` environment variables now trigger a warning, recommending migration to `OPENCLAW_*` names.  This release includes several fixes for channel routing, session management, and agent approvals.  It is recommended to upgrade to benefit from the new features and fixes. No specific prerequisites are required.

features.name: Gateway Service
features.description: The core Gateway process bridges messaging channels to AI agents. Runs as a background daemon service. Single process serves all channels simultaneously.
key_details: openclaw gateway --force — force-kill listener then start
openclaw gateway status — check running status
Managed as system daemon with openclaw gateway start/stop/restart

features.name: Multi-Channel Support
features.description: One Gateway serves multiple messaging channels simultaneously. Each channel connects independently.

features.name: Multi-Agent Routing
features.description: Run multiple named agents. Each agent gets its own session context, model, system prompt, and channel routing. Switch between agents or dispatch tasks.
key_details: Named agents: builder, claw, main, isolated, etc.
Each agent: own model, system prompt, memory, session state
Agent definitions: AGENTS.md + SOUL.md in agent workspace dir
ACP harness sessions via sessions_spawn with runtime:acp

features.name: Agent Skills (AgentSkills Standard)
features.description: Pluggable capabilities using the AgentSkills open standard. Skills are SKILL.md files that inject specialized context when activated.
key_details: Install skills from clawhub.com via the clawhub CLI
SKILL.md format: YAML frontmatter + markdown body
Skills appear in agent system prompt discovery section
openclaw skills list — list installed skills

features.name: Memory System
features.description: Persistent agent memory via MEMORY.md and memory/*.md files. Agents recall prior decisions, preferences, and context across sessions.
key_details: Primary: MEMORY.md in agent workspace root
memory_search tool: semantic search across MEMORY.md + memory/*.md
memory_get tool: targeted line reads from memory files
Agents required to run memory_search before answering questions about prior work

features.name: Session Management
features.description: Full session lifecycle management. Sessions persist across gateway restarts. Multi-session routing per channel.
key_details: openclaw sessions list — list active sessions
sessions_list — list all sessions with filters

features.name: Cron Scheduler
features.description: Gateway-level cron job scheduling for reminders, recurring agent tasks, and timed events.
key_details: cron tool: add, remove, list, run, update jobs
Payload types: systemEvent (main session), agentTurn (isolated session)
sessionTarget: main (systemEvent only) or isolated (agentTurn only)
Webhook delivery: POST finished-run event to delivery.to URL

features.name: Paired Nodes
features.description: Pair external devices (phones, laptops, Raspberry Pi) as nodes. Camera, screen recording, location, notifications, and remote command execution.
key_details: nodes tool: status, describe, camera_snap, screen_record, location_get, notifications_list, run
Pairing via QR code or link
Remote command execution on paired node

features.name: Browser Control
features.description: Full browser automation via OpenClaw Browser Control Server. Profiles: openclaw (isolated) and chrome (relay via extension).
key_details: browser tool: status, start, stop, tabs, open, snapshot, screenshot, act
Profile chrome: Chrome extension relay (existing tabs)
act tool for UI automation: click, type, press, hover, drag, select
snapshot: accessibility tree (aria or role refs)

features.name: Plugin System
features.description: Community plugins that extend Gateway capabilities with new channels, tools, and integrations.
key_details: openclaw plugins list — list installed plugins
Plugins: agent-tools, voice-call, mattermost, zalouser, others

features.name: ACP (Agent Communication Protocol)
features.description: ACP harness integration for spawning and managing external coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode) from within OpenClaw sessions.
key_details: Allowed agents listed via agents_list tool
ACP sessions run in isolated workspace

features.name: Control UI / Dashboard
features.description: Browser-based chat and management dashboard. Opens at http://127.0.0.1:18789/ by default.
key_details: openclaw dashboard — open Control UI in browser
Chat with agents directly in browser (no channel setup needed)

features.name: Secrets Management
features.description: SecretRef contract for runtime secret injection. Secrets applied via openclaw secrets apply, referenced as ${{secret.name}} in config.
key_details: openclaw secrets apply — apply secrets to runtime
Runtime snapshot behavior: secrets not written to disk

features.description: Added message actions for pin, unpin, read, react, and listing reactions in Microsoft Teams.

features.name: Per-Provider Private Network Access
features.description: Added per-provider `models.providers.*.request.allowPrivateNetwork` for trusted self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints, keeping the opt-in scoped to model request surfaces, and refreshing cached WebSocket managers when request transport overrides change.

features.name: Seedance 2.0 Model Refs
features.description: Adds Seedance 2.0 model refs to the bundled fal provider and submits the provider-specific duration, resolution, audio, and seed metadata fields needed for live Seedance 2.0 runs.

features.name: Multipass QA Runner Lane
features.description: Adds a `--runner multipass` lane for `openclaw qa suite` so repo-backed QA scenarios can run inside a disposable Linux VM and write back the usual report, summary, and VM logs.

features.name: ChatGPT Import Ingestion
features.description: Adds ChatGPT import ingestion plus new `Imported Insights` and `Memory Palace` diary subtabs so Dreaming can inspect imported source chats, compiled wiki pages, and full source pages directly from the UI.

features.name: Local Exec Policy Command
features.description: Adds a local `openclaw exec-policy` command with `show`, `preset`, and `set` subcommands for synchronizing requested `tools.exec.*` config with the local exec approvals file, plus follow-up hardening for node-host rejection, rollback safety, and sync conflict detection.

features.name: QA Multipass Runner
features.description: Adds a `--runner multipass` lane for `openclaw qa suite` so repo-backed QA scenarios can run inside a disposable Linux VM and write back the usual report, summary, and VM logs.

features.name: Plugin Manifest Activation and Setup Descriptors
features.description: Allow plugin manifests to declare activation and setup descriptors so plugin setup flows can describe required auth, pairing, and configuration steps without hardcoded core special cases.

features.name: URL-only Generated Asset Delivery
features.description: Add URL-only generated asset delivery for video generation tools.

features.name: Typed Provider Options
features.description: Add typed `providerOptions` for video generation tools.

features.name: Reference Audio Inputs
features.description: Add reference audio inputs for video generation tools.

features.name: Per-Asset Role Hints
features.description: Add per-asset role hints for video generation tools.

features.name: Adaptive Aspect Ratio Support
features.description: Add `adaptive` aspect-ratio support for video generation tools.

features.name: Higher Image Input Cap
features.description: Add a higher image-input cap for video generation tools.

features.name: Imported Insights and Memory Palace Diary Subtabs
features.description: Add `Imported Insights` and `Memory Palace` diary subtabs so Dreaming can inspect imported source chats, compiled wiki pages, and full source pages directly from the UI.

features.name: [embed ...] rich output tag
features.description: Adds the `[embed ...]` rich output tag for embedding external URLs in webchat, gated behind configuration.

features.name: GPT-5.4 vs Opus 4.6 Agentic Parity Report Gate
features.description: Adds the GPT-5.4 vs Opus 4.6 agentic parity report gate with shared scenario coverage checks, stricter evidence heuristics, and skipped-scenario accounting for maintainer review.

features.name: Gemini Text-to-Speech Support
features.description: Adds Gemini text-to-speech support to the bundled `google` plugin.

features.name: Model Auth status card
features.description: Adds a Model Auth status card to the Control UI/Overview, displaying OAuth token health and provider rate-limit pressure. It shows attention callouts when OAuth tokens are expiring or expired. Backed by a new `models.authStatus` gateway method that strips credentials and caches for 60s.

features.name: Cloud storage support for Memory/LanceDB
features.description: Adds cloud storage support to `memory-lancedb` so durable memory indexes can run on remote object storage instead of local disk only.

features.name: GitHub Copilot embedding provider
features.description: Adds a GitHub Copilot embedding provider for memory search, and exposes a dedicated Copilot embedding host helper so plugins can reuse the transport while honoring remote overrides, token refresh, and safer payload validation.

features.name: Agents/local models lean mode
features.description: Adds experimental `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true` to drop heavyweight default tools like `browser`, `cron`, and `message`, reducing prompt size for weaker local-model setups without changing the normal path.

features.name: Gemini Text-to-Speech
features.description: Adds Gemini text-to-speech support to the bundled `google` plugin, including provider registration, voice selection, WAV reply output, PCM telephony output, and setup/docs guidance.

features.name: BlueBubbles Coalesce Same Sender DMs
features.description: Adds opt-in `channels.bluebubbles.coalesceSameSenderDms` to merge split-send DMs (text + URL) into a single agent turn.
key_details: Merges messages not linked via `associatedMessageGuid` within a debounce window.
Debounce window widens from 500ms to 2500ms when enabled.
Merged output is bounded to ≤4000 chars text, ≤20 attachments, and first-plus-latest sampling beyond 10 source entries.

features.name: Skill Workshop plugin
features.description: Adds the Skill Workshop plugin, which captures reusable workflow corrections as pending or auto-applied workspace skills, runs threshold-based reviewer passes for stronger completion bias on reusable procedures, quarantines unsafe proposals, and refreshes skill availability after safe writes.

features.name: Plugin SDK/channels presentation and skills runtime contracts
features.description: Adds presentation and skills runtime contracts, decouples channel presentation rendering, and documents message presentation cards so plugins can own richer interactive surfaces without channel-specific glue.

features.name: QQBot self-contained engine architecture
features.description: Extracts a self-contained engine architecture with QR-code onboarding, native approval handling via `/bot-approve`, per-account resource stacks, credential backup/restore, shared media storage, and unified API/bridge/gateway modules.

features.name: Local Embedded TUI Mode
features.description: Adds a local embedded mode for running terminal chats without a Gateway while keeping plugin approval gates enforced.

features.name: Configurable WhatsApp Native Reply Quoting
features.description: Adds configurable native reply quoting with replyToMode for WhatsApp conversations.

features.name: WhatsApp Group+Direct System Prompt Config
features.description: Forwards per-group and per-direct `systemPrompt` config into inbound context `GroupSystemPrompt` so configured per-chat behavioral instructions are injected on every turn. Supports `"*"` wildcard fallback and account-scoped overrides under `channels.whatsapp.accounts.<id>.{groups,direct}`.

features.name: Agents Sessions List Filters
features.description: Adds mailbox-style `sessions_list` filters for label, agent, and search plus visibility-scoped derived title and last-message previews.

features.name: Operator Personal Identity
features.description: Adds a browser-local personal identity for the operator (name plus local-safe avatar), route user identity rendering through the shared chat/avatar path used by assistant and agent surfaces, and tighten Quick Settings, agent fallback chips, and narrow-screen chat layouts so personalization no longer wastes space or clips controls.

features.name: Gateway Diagnostics Export
features.description: Enables payload-free stability recording by default and add a support-ready diagnostics export with sanitized logs, status, health, config, and stability snapshots for bug reports.

features.name: Agents Trajectory Export
features.description: Adds default-on local trajectory capture plus `/export-trajectory` bundles with redacted transcripts, runtime events, prompts, metadata, and artifacts for reproducible debugging and dataset export.

features.name: Tencent Cloud Provider Plugin
features.description: Adds the bundled Tencent Cloud provider plugin with TokenHub onboarding, docs, `hy3-preview` model catalog entries, and tiered Hy3 pricing metadata.

features.name: Tokenjuice Plugin
features.description: Adds bundled native OpenClaw support for tokenjuice as an opt-in plugin that compacts noisy `exec` and `bash` tool results in Pi embedded runs.

features.name: OpenClaw Tools MCP Bridge
features.description: Adds an explicit `openClawToolsMcpBridge` option that injects a core OpenClaw MCP server for selected built-in tools, starting with `cron`.

features.name: CLI Debug Timing Helper
features.description: Adds an opt-in temporary debug timing helper for local CLI performance investigations, with readable stderr output, JSONL capture, and docs for removing probes before landing fixes.

features.name: Thai Translation Support
features.description: Adds Thai translation support for the docs site.

features.name: xAI Image, Text, and Speech Support
features.description: Adds image generation, text-to-speech, and speech-to-text support for xAI, including `grok-imagine-image` / `grok-imagine-image-pro`, reference-image edits, six live xAI voices, MP3/WAV/PCM/G.711 TTS formats, `grok-stt` audio transcription, and xAI realtime transcription for Voice Call streaming.
key_details: Provides six live xAI voices for TTS.
Supports MP3, WAV, PCM, and G.711 TTS formats.
Enables xAI realtime transcription for Voice Call streaming.

features.name: Voice Call Streaming Transcription
features.description: Adds Voice Call streaming transcription for Deepgram, ElevenLabs, and Mistral, alongside the existing OpenAI and xAI realtime STT paths; ElevenLabs also gains Scribe v2 batch audio transcription for inbound media.
key_details: Supports Voice Call streaming transcription for Deepgram, ElevenLabs, and Mistral.
ElevenLabs gains Scribe v2 batch audio transcription for inbound media.

features.name: Auto-Install Missing Plugins
features.description: Auto-installs missing provider and channel plugins during setup so first-run configuration can complete without manual plugin recovery.

features.name: OpenAI Native Web Search
features.description: Uses OpenAI's native `web_search` tool automatically for direct OpenAI Responses models when web search is enabled and no managed search provider is pinned; explicit providers such as Brave keep the managed `web_search` tool.

features.name: Configurable Native Reply Quoting
features.description: Adds configurable native reply quoting with replyToMode for WhatsApp conversations.

features.name: Mailbox-Style Sessions List Filters
features.description: Adds mailbox-style `sessions_list` filters for label, agent, and search plus visibility-scoped derived title and last-message previews.

features.name: Payload-Free Stability Recording
features.description: Enables payload-free stability recording by default and add a support-ready diagnostics export with sanitized logs, status, health, config, and stability snapshots for bug reports.

features.name: Local Trajectory Capture
features.description: Adds default-on local trajectory capture plus `/export-trajectory` bundles with redacted transcripts, runtime events, prompts, metadata, and artifacts for reproducible debugging and dataset export.

features.name: Claude Opus 4.7 Mantle Support
features.description: Adds Claude Opus 4.7 through Mantle's Anthropic Messages route with provider-owned bearer-auth streaming, so the model is actually callable without treating AWS bearer tokens like Anthropic API keys.

features.name: GPT-5 Prompt Overlay
features.description: Moves the GPT-5 prompt overlay into the shared provider runtime so compatible GPT-5 models receive the same behavior and heartbeat guidance through OpenAI, OpenRouter, OpenCode, Codex, and other GPT providers; add `agents.defaults.promptOverlays.gpt5.personality` as the global friendly-style toggle while keeping the OpenAI plugin setting as a fallback.
key_details: Shared provider runtime for GPT-5 prompt overlay.

features.name: xAI Image Generation, Text-to-Speech, and Speech-to-Text Support
features.description: Adds image generation, text-to-speech, and speech-to-text support to the xAI provider, including `grok-imagine-image` / `grok-imagine-image-pro`, reference-image edits, six live xAI voices, MP3/WAV/PCM/G.711 TTS formats, `grok-stt` audio transcription, and xAI realtime transcription for Voice Call streaming.
key_details: xAI realtime transcription for Voice Call streaming

features.name: Voice Call Streaming Transcription for Deepgram, ElevenLabs, and Mistral
features.description: Adds Voice Call streaming transcription for Deepgram, ElevenLabs, and Mistral, alongside the existing OpenAI and xAI realtime STT paths; ElevenLabs also gains Scribe v2 batch audio transcription for inbound media.

features.name: Auto-install Missing Provider and Channel Plugins During Setup
features.description: Auto-installs missing provider and channel plugins during setup so first-run configuration can complete without manual plugin recovery.

features.name: /models add Command
features.description: Adds `/models add <provider> <modelId>` so you can register a model from chat and use it without restarting the gateway; keep `/models` as a simple provider browser while adding clearer add guidance and copy-friendly command examples.
key_details: Provides clearer add guidance and copy-friendly command examples.

features.name: Image generation quality and output format hints
features.description: Agents can now request provider-supported quality and output format hints for image generation, and pass OpenAI-specific background, moderation, compression, and user hints through the `image_generate` tool.

features.name: Forked context for native sessions_spawn runs
features.description: Adds optional forked context for native `sessions_spawn` runs so agents can let a child inherit the requester transcript when needed, while keeping clean isolated sessions as the default; includes prompt guidance, context-engine hook metadata, docs, and QA coverage.

features.name: Per-call timeoutMs support for generation tools
features.description: Adds optional per-call `timeoutMs` support for image, video, music, and TTS generation tools so agents can extend provider request timeouts only when a specific generation needs it.

features.name: Configurable memorySearch.local.contextSize
features.description: Adds configurable `memorySearch.local.contextSize` with a 4096 default so local embedding contexts can be tuned for constrained hosts without patching the memory host.

features.name: Google Meet Participant Plugin
features.description: Bundled participant plugin for Google Meet, including personal Google auth, Chrome/Twilio realtime sessions, paired-node Chrome support, artifact/attendance exports, and recovery tooling for already-open Meet tabs.
key_details: Recovery tooling for already-open Meet tabs

features.name: DeepSeek V4 Flash and V4 Pro
features.description: DeepSeek V4 Flash and V4 Pro are now included in the bundled catalog. V4 Flash is the onboarding default. DeepSeek thinking/replay behavior is fixed for follow-up tool-call turns.

V4 Flash is the onboarding default
DeepSeek thinking/replay behavior fixed for follow-up tool-call turns

features.name: Realtime Voice Loops

Voice loops consult the full OpenClaw agent for tool-backed answers

features.name: Browser Automation Improvements

features.name: Lighter Plugin and Model Infrastructure

Artifact and attendance workflows for conference records, recordings, transcripts, smart notes, and participant sessions

features.name: Gemini Live Realtime Voice Provider

features.name: Gradium Text-to-Speech Provider
features.description: A bundled text-to-speech provider with voice-note and telephony output support.

features.name: Document Extract Plugin
features.description: Local PDF extraction is moved into a bundled `document-extract` plugin.

features.name: Bonjour Plugin
features.description: LAN Gateway discovery advertising is moved into a default-enabled bundled plugin.

features.name: Browser Automation Skill
features.description: A `browser-automation` skill is bundled with the multi-step snapshot, stable-tab, stale-ref, and manual-blocker loop for agent-controlled pages.

features.name: Cerebras Bundled Plugin
features.description: Adds Cerebras as a bundled plugin with onboarding, static model catalog, docs, and manifest-owned endpoint metadata.

features.name: Matrix E2EE Setup Command
features.description: Adds `openclaw matrix encryption setup` to enable Matrix encryption, bootstrap recovery, and print verification status from one setup flow.

features.name: Claude Importer
features.description: Adds a bundled Claude importer that previews and applies Claude Code and Claude Desktop instructions, MCP servers, skills, command prompts, and safe archive/manual-review state.

features.name: Hermes Importer
features.description: Adds `openclaw migrate` with plan, dry-run, JSON, pre-migration backup, onboarding detection, archive-only report copies, and a bundled Hermes importer for configuration, memory/plugin hints, model providers, MCP servers, skills, and supported credentials.

features.name: Agents Compaction MaxActiveTranscriptBytes
features.description: Adds an opt-in `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes` preflight trigger that runs normal local compaction when the active JSONL grows too large, requiring transcript rotation so successful compaction moves future turns onto a smaller successor file instead of raw byte-splitting history.

features.name: Generic Browser Realtime Transport Contract
features.description: Adds a generic browser realtime transport contract, Google Live browser Talk sessions with constrained ephemeral tokens, and a Gateway relay for backend-only realtime voice plugins.

features.name: Asymmetric Embedding Endpoints
features.description: Adds optional `memorySearch.inputType`, `queryInputType`, and `documentInputType` config for asymmetric embedding endpoints, including direct query embeddings and provider batch indexing.

features.name: Model-Specific Retrieval Query Prefixes
features.description: Adds model-specific retrieval query prefixes for `nomic-embed-text`, `qwen3-embedding`, and `mxbai-embed-large` memory-search queries while leaving document batches unchanged.

features.name: Raw Config Pending-Changes Diff Panel
features.description: Adds a raw config pending-changes diff panel that parses JSON5, redacts sensitive values until reveal, and avoids fake raw-edit callbacks when opening the panel.

features.name: Preflight Compaction Trigger
features.description: Adds an opt-in `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes` preflight trigger that runs normal local compaction when the active JSONL grows too large, requiring transcript rotation so successful compaction moves future turns onto a smaller successor file instead of raw byte-splitting history.

features.name: Typed `cron_changed` Hook
features.description: Adds a typed `cron_changed` hook for observing gateway-owned cron lifecycle updates without depending on internal cron events.

features.name: Operator-managed outbound proxy routing
features.description: Adds opt-in operator-managed outbound proxy routing with strict http:// forward-proxy validation, loopback-only Gateway bypass, and cleanup of proxy env/dispatcher state on exit.

features.name: Codex Computer Use setup
features.description: Adds Computer Use setup for Codex-mode agents, including /codex computer-use status/install, marketplace discovery, optional auto-install, and fail-closed MCP server checks before Codex-mode turns start.

features.name: Tencent Yuanbao external channel plugin
features.description: Registers the Tencent Yuanbao external channel plugin in the official channel catalog, contract suites, and community plugin docs, with a new docs/channels/yuanbao.md quick-start guide for WebSocket bot DMs and group chats.

features.name: QQBot full group chat support
features.description: Adds full group chat support (history tracking, @-mention gating, activation modes, per-group config, FIFO message queue with deliver debounce), C2C stream_messages streaming with a StreamingController lifecycle manager, unified sendMedia with chunked upload for large files, and refactor the engine into pipeline stages, focused outbound submodules, builtin slash-command modules, and explicit DI ports via createEngineAdapters().

features.name: DeepInfra provider
features.description: Adds a bundled DeepInfra provider with DEEPINFRA_API_KEY onboarding, dynamic OpenAI-compatible model discovery, image generation/editing, image/audio media understanding, TTS, text-to-video, memory embeddings, static catalog metadata, and provider-owned base URL policy.

features.name: Versioned structured approval metadata to Matrix
features.description: Attaches versioned structured approval metadata to pending approval messages so capable Matrix clients can render richer approval UI while body text and reaction fallback keep working.

features.name: DeepInfra bundled provider
features.description: DeepInfra joins the bundled provider set with model discovery, media generation/editing, TTS, embeddings, and provider-owned onboarding policy.

features.name: Tencent Yuanbao and QQBot channel coverage
features.description: Tencent Yuanbao and QQBot support expand channel coverage with Yuanbao docs/catalog entries and QQBot group chat, streaming, media upload, and pipeline refactors.

features.name: Manifest-first metadata
features.description: Plugin startup and model catalogs move toward manifest-first metadata, reducing Gateway boot work and making provider rows/aliases/suppressions easier to audit.

breaking_changes.title: Escape invisible Unicode format characters in approval prompts
breaking_changes.description: Invisible Unicode format characters in approval prompts are now escaped so zero-width command text renders as visible `\u{...}` escapes instead of spoofing the reviewed command.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Normalize compatibility Unicode and strip invisible formatting code points before obfuscation checks
breaking_changes.description: Compatibility Unicode is now normalized and invisible formatting code points are stripped before obfuscation checks, so zero-width and fullwidth command tricks no longer suppress heuristic detection.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Preserve POSIX case sensitivity and keep `?` within a single path segment in exec allowlist
breaking_changes.description: POSIX case sensitivity is now preserved and `?` is kept within a single path segment, so exact-looking allowlist patterns no longer overmatch executables across case or directory boundaries.
breaking_changes.migration: Update allowlist patterns to be case-sensitive and ensure `?` only matches within a single path segment.

breaking_changes.title: Require sender ownership for `/config` and `/debug` commands
breaking_changes.description: Authorized non-owner senders can no longer reach owner-only config and runtime debug surfaces via `/config` and `/debug` commands. Only the owner can use these commands.
breaking_changes.migration: Only the owner can use `/config` and `/debug` commands.

breaking_changes.title: Clear unbound client-declared scopes on shared-token WebSocket connects
breaking_changes.description: Device-less shared-token operators cannot self-declare elevated scopes on shared-token WebSocket connects.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway Tool/Exec Config Restrictions
breaking_changes.description: Model-facing `gateway config.apply` and `config.patch` writes are now blocked from changing exec approval paths such as `safeBins`, `safeBinProfiles`, `safeBinTrustedDirs`, and `strictInlineEval`. This prevents malicious models from modifying security-sensitive configurations.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that exec approval paths are managed through secure channels and not directly exposed to model-driven configuration updates.

breaking_changes.title: Host Exec/Env Sanitization
breaking_changes.description: Dangerous Java, Rust, Cargo, Git, Kubernetes, cloud credential, config-path, and Helm env overrides are now blocked. This prevents host-run tools from being redirected to attacker-chosen code, config, credentials, or repository state.
breaking_changes.migration: Review and adjust any existing configurations that rely on these environment variables. Ensure that tools are not dependent on potentially malicious environment overrides.

breaking_changes.title: Network Fetch Guard: Redirect Bodies Dropped
breaking_changes.description: Request bodies and body-describing headers are now dropped on cross-origin `307` and `308` redirects by default. This prevents attacker-controlled redirect hops from receiving secret-bearing POST payloads from SSRF-guarded fetch flows unless a caller explicitly opts in.
breaking_changes.migration: If your application relies on forwarding request bodies across redirects, you will need to explicitly opt-in to this behavior. Review your fetch configurations and ensure that you understand the security implications of forwarding request bodies across redirects.

breaking_changes.title: Browser Node Invoke: Profile Mutations Blocked
breaking_changes.description: Persistent browser profile create, reset, and delete mutations through `browser.proxy` are now blocked on both gateway-forwarded `node.invoke` and the node-host proxy path, even when no profile allowlist is configured.
breaking_changes.migration: If your application relies on creating, resetting, or deleting browser profiles through `browser.proxy`, you will need to adjust your approach. Consider using alternative methods for managing browser profiles that do not involve persistent mutations.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway Auth: WebSocket Invalidation
breaking_changes.description: Existing shared-token and password WebSocket sessions are now invalidated when the configured secret rotates. This ensures that stale authenticated sockets cannot stay attached after token or password changes.
breaking_changes.migration: No specific migration steps are required. This change improves security by ensuring that WebSocket sessions are properly invalidated after authentication secrets are rotated.

breaking_changes.title: Agents/gateway-tool: Reject config.patch and config.apply calls that enable security-sensitive flags
breaking_changes.description: The model-facing gateway tool now rejects `config.patch` and `config.apply` calls that would newly enable any flag enumerated by `openclaw security audit` (e.g., `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth`, `allowInsecureAuth`, `dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback`, `hooks.gmail.allowUnsafeExternalContent`, `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly: false`). This prevents models from escalating privileges through the gateway tool.
affected: Users relying on the model-facing gateway tool to enable security-sensitive flags.
breaking_changes.migration: Already-enabled flags pass through unchanged, so non-dangerous edits in the same patch still apply. Direct authenticated operator RPC behavior is unchanged. Review your gateway tool configurations and ensure that no security-sensitive flags are being enabled through the model-facing gateway tool.

breaking_changes.title: Browser/SSRF: Preserve explicit strict browser navigation mode for legacy configs
breaking_changes.description: Explicit strict browser navigation mode for legacy `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork: false` configs is now preserved by normalizing the legacy alias to the canonical strict marker instead of silently widening those installs to the default non-strict hostname-navigation path. This ensures that existing strict SSRF policies are not unintentionally relaxed.
breaking_changes.migration: No direct migration steps are required, but verify that your browser SSRF policy remains as intended after the upgrade.

breaking_changes.title: Browser SSRF Policy Normalization
breaking_changes.description: Explicit strict browser navigation mode for legacy `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork: false` configs is now normalized to the canonical strict marker instead of silently widening those installs to the default non-strict hostname-navigation path. This ensures that existing configurations that explicitly disabled private network access for browser navigation continue to do so.
breaking_changes.migration: If you relied on the previous behavior where `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork: false` implicitly allowed hostname navigation, you may need to explicitly configure the desired SSRF policy.

breaking_changes.title: Feishu Allowlist Canonicalization
breaking_changes.description: Feishu allowlist entries are now canonicalized by explicit `user`/`chat` kind, repeated `feishu:`/`lark:` provider prefixes are stripped, and opaque Feishu IDs are no longer folded to lowercase. This prevents allowlist matching from crossing user/chat namespaces or widening to case-insensitive ID matches the operator did not intend.
breaking_changes.migration: Review your Feishu allowlist entries to ensure they are correctly specified with explicit `user`/`chat` kinds and that opaque IDs are case-sensitive. Remove any redundant `feishu:`/`lark:` prefixes.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway Tool Config Patch Security

affected: Users using the model-facing gateway tool to patch configurations.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that any necessary flags are enabled through direct authenticated operator RPC calls instead of the model-facing gateway tool.

breaking_changes.description: Anchors trusted local `MEDIA:` tool-result passthrough on the exact raw name of this run's registered built-in tools. Rejects client tool definitions whose names normalize-collide with a built-in or with another client tool in the same request (`400 invalid_request_error` on both JSON and SSE paths).

breaking_changes.migration: Ensure client-supplied tool names do not collide with built-in tool names or other client-supplied tool names in the same request.

breaking_changes.title: Dreaming storage mode change
breaking_changes.description: The default `dreaming.storage.mode` has changed from `inline` to `separate`. Dreaming phase blocks (`## Light Sleep`, `## REM Sleep`) now land in `memory/dreaming/{phase}/YYYY-MM-DD.md` instead of being injected into `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.
breaking_changes.migration: Operators who want the previous behavior can opt in by setting `plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.storage.mode: "inline"`.

breaking_changes.title: Dreaming storage mode changed to separate

breaking_changes.title: Memory QMD workspace file read restriction
breaking_changes.description: The QMD memory backend can no longer be used as a generic workspace-file read shim that bypasses `read` tool-policy denials. `memory_get` now rejects reads of arbitrary workspace markdown paths and only allows canonical memory files (`MEMORY.md`, `memory.md`, `DREAMS.md`, `dreams.md`, `memory/**`) plus exact paths of active indexed QMD workspace documents.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure `memory_get` calls only target canonical memory files or active indexed QMD workspace documents.

breaking_changes.title: Auth/commands: require owner identity for owner-enforced commands
breaking_changes.description: Requires owner identity (an owner-candidate match or internal `operator.admin`) for owner-enforced commands instead of treating wildcard channel `allowFrom` or empty owner-candidate lists as sufficient, so non-owner senders can no longer reach owner-only commands through a permissive fallback when `enforceOwnerForCommands=true` and `commands.ownerAllowFrom` is unset.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that owner-enforced commands are only accessible to authorized users by properly configuring owner candidates or using the `operator.admin` role.

breaking_changes.title: Control UI/CSP: tighten `img-src` to `'self' data:` only
breaking_changes.description: Tightens `img-src` to `'self' data:` only, and makes Control UI avatar helpers drop remote `http(s)` and protocol-relative URLs so the UI falls back to the built-in logo/badge instead of issuing arbitrary remote image fetches. Same-origin avatar routes (relative paths) and `data:image/...` avatars still render.
breaking_changes.migration: Use same-origin avatar routes (relative paths) or `data:image/...` avatars for Control UI avatars.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/Control UI: require gateway auth on the Control UI avatar route
breaking_changes.description: Requires gateway auth on the Control UI avatar route (`GET /avatar/<agentId>` and `?meta=1` metadata) when auth is configured, matching the sibling assistant-media route, and propagate the existing gateway token through the UI avatar fetch (bearer header + authenticated blob URL) so authenticated dashboards still load local avatars.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure gateway authentication is configured for the Control UI avatar route.

breaking_changes.title: Exec/allowlist: reject POSIX parameter expansion forms in unquoted heredocs
breaking_changes.description: Rejects POSIX parameter expansion forms such as `$VAR`, `$?`, `$$`, `$1`, and `$@` inside unquoted heredocs during shell approval analysis, so these heredocs no longer pass allowlist review as plain text.
breaking_changes.migration: Avoid using POSIX parameter expansion forms in unquoted heredocs during shell approval analysis.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/MCP loopback: derive owner-only tool visibility from distinct authenticated owner
breaking_changes.description: Derives owner-only tool visibility from distinct authenticated owner vs non-owner loopback bearers instead of the caller-controlled owner header, so non-owner MCP child processes cannot recover owner access by spoofing request metadata.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that MCP child processes cannot recover owner access by spoofing request metadata.

breaking_changes.title: Control UI avatar CSP restrictions
breaking_changes.description: The Control UI's `img-src` CSP has been tightened to `'self' data:`. Remote `http(s)` and protocol-relative URLs for avatars are now dropped, falling back to the built-in logo/badge. Same-origin avatar routes (relative paths) and `data:image/...` avatars still render.
breaking_changes.migration: Use same-origin avatar routes (relative paths) or `data:image/...` avatars to ensure they render correctly in the Control UI.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/MCP loopback owner visibility
breaking_changes.description: Owner-only tool visibility is now derived from distinct authenticated owner vs non-owner loopback bearers instead of the caller-controlled owner header. Non-owner MCP child processes can no longer recover owner access by spoofing request metadata.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that MCP child processes are correctly authenticated to maintain owner access.

breaking_changes.title: OpenShell sandbox write restrictions
breaking_changes.description: Host-side sandbox writes are now pinned under the mounted root. Symlink-parent rebinds can no longer redirect `writeFile` outside the workspace during local mirror updates.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that sandbox writes remain within the mounted root to prevent redirection outside the workspace.

breaking_changes.title: Owner identity required for owner-enforced commands
breaking_changes.description: Owner identity (an owner-candidate match or internal `operator.admin`) is now required for owner-enforced commands instead of treating wildcard channel `allowFrom` or empty owner-candidate lists as sufficient. Non-owner senders can no longer reach owner-only commands through a permissive fallback when `enforceOwnerForCommands=true` and `commands.ownerAllowFrom` is unset.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that owner-enforced commands are only accessible to authorized users by properly configuring owner candidates or internal admin roles.

breaking_changes.title: Tightened Control UI CSP
breaking_changes.description: The `img-src` CSP directive in the Control UI has been tightened to `'self' data:` only. Remote `http(s)` and protocol-relative URLs for avatars are now dropped, causing the UI to fall back to the built-in logo/badge instead of issuing arbitrary remote image fetches. Same-origin avatar routes (relative paths) and `data:image/...` avatars still render.
breaking_changes.migration: Use same-origin avatar routes (relative paths) or `data:image/...` avatars to ensure avatars are displayed correctly in the Control UI.

breaking_changes.title: Owner-only tool visibility derived from authenticated owner vs non-owner loopback bearers
breaking_changes.description: Owner-only tool visibility is now derived from distinct authenticated owner vs non-owner loopback bearers instead of the caller-controlled owner header. Non-owner MCP child processes cannot recover owner access by spoofing request metadata.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that MCP child processes are properly authenticated to access owner-only tools.

breaking_changes.title: POSIX parameter expansion forms rejected in unquoted heredocs during shell approval analysis
breaking_changes.description: POSIX parameter expansion forms such as `$VAR`, `$?`, `$$`, `$1`, and `$@` are now rejected inside unquoted heredocs during shell approval analysis, so these heredocs no longer pass allowlist review as plain text.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that heredocs do not contain POSIX parameter expansion forms or are properly quoted.

breaking_changes.title: Codex CLI Auth Import Removed
breaking_changes.description: The Codex CLI auth import path has been removed from onboarding and provider discovery. OpenClaw no longer copies `~/.codex` OAuth material into agent auth stores.
affected: Users who previously relied on the automatic import of Codex CLI authentication.
breaking_changes.migration: Use browser login or device pairing instead.

breaking_changes.title: Legacy Codex App Server Guardian Shortcut Removed
breaking_changes.description: The legacy `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_GUARDIAN` shortcut is removed; use plugin config `appServer.mode: "guardian"` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_MODE=guardian`.
affected: Users who used the `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_GUARDIAN` environment variable to configure the Codex app server mode.
breaking_changes.migration: Use plugin config `appServer.mode: "guardian"` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_MODE=guardian` instead.

breaking_changes.title: Legacy Codex App Server Guardian Shortcut Removed
breaking_changes.description: The legacy `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_GUARDIAN` shortcut is removed; use plugin config `appServer.mode: "guardian"` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_MODE=guardian`.
affected: Users who used the `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_GUARDIAN` environment variable to configure Codex app server guardian mode.
breaking_changes.migration: Use plugin config `appServer.mode: "guardian"` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_MODE=guardian` instead.

breaking_changes.title: Codex CLI Auth Import Removed
breaking_changes.description: The Codex CLI auth import path has been removed from onboarding and provider discovery. OpenClaw no longer copies `~/.codex` OAuth material into agent auth stores.
affected: Users who previously relied on the automatic import of Codex CLI authentication
breaking_changes.migration: Use browser login or device pairing instead.

breaking_changes.title: Codex App Server Guardian Mode
breaking_changes.description: The legacy `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_GUARDIAN` shortcut is removed; use plugin config `appServer.mode: "guardian"` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_MODE=guardian`.
breaking_changes.migration: Use plugin config `appServer.mode: "guardian"` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_MODE=guardian` instead of `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_GUARDIAN`.

breaking_changes.title: Restrict Agent-Driven Gateway Configuration Edits
breaking_changes.description: Agent-driven `gateway config.apply`/`config.patch` runtime edits are now restricted to a narrow set of agent-tunable prompt, model, and mention-gating paths (including Telegram topic-level `requireMention`). This prevents privilege escalation by limiting the ability of agents to modify sensitive configuration keys.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that agents only attempt to modify the allowlisted configuration paths. Review agent behavior and adjust configuration update logic as needed.

breaking_changes.title: Immediate Webhook Secret Revocation
breaking_changes.description: Webhook route secrets backed by `SecretRef` are now re-resolved on each request. This ensures that `openclaw secrets reload` immediately revokes the previous secret, instead of waiting for a gateway restart.
breaking_changes.migration: No specific migration steps are required. This change enhances security by ensuring immediate secret revocation.

breaking_changes.title: Codex Auth Path Migration
breaking_changes.description: Codex ACP, Codex app-server, and Codex CLI runs no longer materialize `auth.json` bridge files. Codex-owned runtimes now use their normal `CODEX_HOME`/`~/.codex` auth path directly.
breaking_changes.migration: Remove any reliance on the `auth.json` bridge file for Codex authentication. Ensure that Codex runtimes are configured to use the `CODEX_HOME` or `~/.codex` auth path.

breaking_changes.title: Android Cleartext Gateway Connection Restriction
breaking_changes.description: Loopback-only cleartext gateway connections on Android manual and scanned routes are now required. Private-LAN and link-local `ws://` endpoints will now fail closed unless TLS is enabled.
breaking_changes.migration: Enable TLS for private-LAN and link-local gateway connections on Android, or use loopback-only connections.

breaking_changes.title: Mobile Pairing Security Enhancement
breaking_changes.description: Cleartext mobile pairing now requires private-IP or loopback hosts. `.local` or dotless hostnames are no longer treated as safe cleartext endpoints.
breaking_changes.migration: Use private-IP or loopback hosts for cleartext mobile pairing. Avoid using `.local` or dotless hostnames.

breaking_changes.title: Plugin Setup API Security Restriction
breaking_changes.description: Setup-api lookup no longer falls back to the launch directory. Workspace-local `extensions/<plugin>/setup-api.*` files cannot be executed during provider setup resolution.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that plugin setup APIs are resolved correctly without relying on the launch directory fallback.

breaking_changes.title: Explicit Chat Exec-Approval Enablement
breaking_changes.description: Explicit chat exec-approval enablement is now required instead of auto-enabling approval clients just because approvers resolve from config or owner allowlists.
breaking_changes.migration: Explicitly enable chat exec-approval for the desired clients.

breaking_changes.title: Discord Channel Policy Security Enhancement
breaking_changes.description: Native slash-command channel policy no longer bypasses configured owner or member restrictions, while preserving channel-policy fallback when no stricter access rule exists.
breaking_changes.migration: Review Discord channel policies to ensure they align with intended access restrictions.

breaking_changes.title: Android ASK_OPENCLAW Intent Security Enhancement
breaking_changes.description: `ASK_OPENCLAW` intents no longer auto-send injected prompts. External app actions only prefill the draft instead of dispatching it immediately.
breaking_changes.migration: External apps using `ASK_OPENCLAW` intents will now only prefill the draft instead of sending it immediately. Users will need to manually send the prompt.

breaking_changes.title: MCP Tools Security Restriction
breaking_changes.description: The ACPX OpenClaw tools bridge no longer lists or invokes owner-only tools such as `cron`, closing a privilege-escalation path for non-owner MCP callers.
breaking_changes.migration: Non-owner MCP callers will no longer be able to list or invoke owner-only tools.

breaking_changes.title: Teams Bot Framework Token Replay Prevention
breaking_changes.description: Shared Bot Framework audience tokens must now name the configured Teams app via verified `appid` or `azp`, blocking cross-bot token replay on the global audience.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that shared Bot Framework audience tokens include the verified `appid` or `azp` of the configured Teams app.

breaking_changes.title: Restrict Agent-Driven Gateway Configuration Edits
breaking_changes.description: Agent-driven `gateway config.apply`/`config.patch` runtime edits are now restricted to a narrow set of agent-tunable prompt, model, and mention-gating paths. This change prevents agents from modifying sensitive configuration keys, enhancing system security.
breaking_changes.migration: Review agent configurations that modify gateway settings and ensure they only target the allowlisted paths. Adjust agent logic to comply with the new restrictions.

breaking_changes.title: Plugin SDK/tool-result transforms
breaking_changes.description: The Pi-only `api.registerEmbeddedExtensionFactory(...)` compatibility path is removed. Bundled tool-result rewrites must use `api.registerAgentToolResultMiddleware(...)` with `contracts.agentToolResultMiddleware` declaring the targeted harnesses.
breaking_changes.migration: Use `api.registerAgentToolResultMiddleware(...)` with `contracts.agentToolResultMiddleware` declaring the targeted harnesses.

breaking_changes.title: Plugin Config Load/Write Helpers Deprecation
breaking_changes.description: Direct plugin config load/write helpers are deprecated in favor of passed runtime snapshots plus transactional mutation helpers with explicit restart follow-up policy, scanner guardrails, runtime warnings, and revision-based cache invalidation.
breaking_changes.migration: Use passed runtime snapshots and transactional mutation helpers instead of direct config load/write helpers.

breaking_changes.title: Deprecated Implicit Startup Sidecar Loading
breaking_changes.description: Implicit startup sidecar loading for plugins is deprecated. Plugins should migrate to explicit `activation.onStartup` metadata.
breaking_changes.migration: Update plugin manifests to include `activation.onStartup` metadata.

breaking_changes.title: Automatic Oversized sessions.json Rotation Backups Removed
breaking_changes.description: Automatic oversized `sessions.json` rotation backups have been removed. The `session.maintenance.rotateBytes` setting is deprecated and ignored.
breaking_changes.migration: Remove `session.maintenance.rotateBytes` from your configuration. Use `openclaw doctor --fix` to remove the ignored key.
